name: Hologram OS
description: The Hologram OS agent endpoint — a near feature-complete MCP server projecting every holospace as a self-verifying UOR resource, with a built-in verify_object tool no ordinary MCP server has.

skills.name: verify_object
skills.description: Re-derive a UOR object's did from its content and report whether it self-verifies (Law L5).

skills.name: resolve_object
skills.description: Resolve ANY reference to its self-verifying UOR object: a did:holo / holo:// resource from the store, OR an open-web identifier (DOI, ISBN, Wikidata Q-id, place, GitHub owner/repo, gbif:/chem: prefix, domain, @user@host) resolved live over open APIs (Holo Resolve, no AI). Re-derive its did to verify (Law L5).

skills.name: search_web
skills.description: Federated search across the open web (Wikipedia · Wikidata · Crossref · Open Library · OpenStreetMap) — one query, fused by Reciprocal Rank Fusion + reconciled to Wikidata, no AI. Each result is re-derivable via resolve_object.

skills.name: answer
skills.description: A corroborated, conflict-aware answer composed DETERMINISTICALLY from the open web (no AI — it cannot hallucinate; it asserts only what verifiable sources agree on). Every fact traces to a source; disagreements are flagged, not merged. The query is governed by the constitution's perimeter.

skills.name: ask_model
skills.description: Ask the connected agent's OWN model a question (MCP sampling — the INVERSE direction: a holospace borrows the agent's intelligence). Requires a sampling-capable client.

skills.name: ask_user
skills.description: Ask the human, via the agent host, for input (MCP elicitation). Requires an elicitation-capable client.

skills.name: list_roots
skills.description: List the file:// roots the agent host has granted access to (MCP roots). Requires a roots-capable client.

skills.name: verify_batch
skills.description: Verify many UOR objects at once; reports progress and is cancellable.

skills.name: holo_rank
skills.description: Personal, content-addressed PageRank (HoloRank). Given reference edges {rel,from,to,by?,weight?} and a seed (teleport set of did:holo you trust/own), returns a SELF-VERIFYING UOR ranking object. The result is deterministic, so DON'T trust it — re-derive its did (verify_object, Law L5) and/or recompute holo_rank over the same edges to confirm the same κ. Trustless ranking for agents.

skills.name: holo_recommend
skills.description: One round-trip personal recommendations. Give the apps/did:holo you use ('usage'); the server fetches only YOUR neighbourhood by content address (bounded — taste-aligned reviewers + their picks, not the whole corpus) and ranks it, returning recs (each with a reason) plus a SELF-VERIFYING UOR result you re-derive (Law L5) and can recompute (deterministic). Requires the server to be wired to a review corpus.

skills.name: disclose_for_purpose
skills.description: Holo Privacy: obtain a W3C Verifiable Presentation that discloses ONLY the personal-data claims the user's standing policy authorizes for a given DPV purpose + recipient (IETF SD-JWT selective disclosure) — and can prove a fact ("over 18") without revealing the value behind it. Returns null when nothing is authorized (default-deny). Needs a connected wallet (the Holo Privacy app). Present the result to any tool that handles personal data.

skills.name: verify_disclosure
skills.description: Holo Privacy: verify a W3C Verifiable Presentation — re-derive its content address (Law L5), verify the holder's data-integrity proof, recompute every disclosed SD-JWT digest, and (if the data commitment is given) confirm membership. Reports what was disclosed and how much (n of m). Don't trust a disclosure you didn't verify.

skills.name: browser_open
skills.description: Open a URL in Holo Browser's content-addressed loader and return a self-verifying snapshot: the served/minted κ (a did:holo you re-derive, Law L5), final URL, title, extracted text and links. Accepts holo://κ, ipfs://, ENS .eth and http(s) (a live page is fetched once and minted into a κ).

skills.name: browser_fetch
skills.description: Fetch a URL through Holo Browser's loading seam and return its content address (κ / did:holo) plus content-type and size, re-derived (Law L5) — the bytes are content-addressed substrate, not a trusted response.

skills.name: read_liberty
skills.description: Return a self-verifying chapter of On Liberty as a UOR object the agent can re-derive (Law L5). Args: chapter (0=Introduction, 1..5 = the chapters) or find (a phrase; returns the chapter containing it). With no args, returns the book root object.

skills.name: standing_term
skills.description: Return the user's current standing term (the relationship agreement they propose to every app) as a content-addressed roster object.

skills.name: agreement_for
skills.description: Return the signed agreement record for an app (the granted capabilities under the user's terms), or null. Args: appId.

skills.name: wallet_list_chains
skills.description: List the blockchains Holo Wallet supports and your address on each (BIP-44 / SLIP-0010 derived; re-derivable by the agent — Law L5). Implemented by mcp/holo-wallet-mcp.mjs.

skills.name: wallet_get_address
skills.description: The wallet address for a chain (e.g. 'ethereum','bitcoin','solana'). Deterministic from the seed; verify by re-derivation (Law L5).

skills.name: wallet_get_balance
skills.description: The native-token balance for a chain (reads live from public RPC/Esplora).

skills.name: wallet_get_portfolio
skills.description: Addresses and native balances across every supported chain.

skills.name: wallet_quote
skills.description: Quote the cost of a send (fee estimate). Does NOT sign or broadcast anything.

skills.name: wallet_sign_message
skills.description: Sign a message with the wallet key for a chain. GATED: requires explicit human approval (default-deny).

skills.name: wallet_send
skills.description: Send / transfer value. GATED: signs and broadcasts ONLY after explicit human approval (default-deny). Quote first with wallet_quote.
